title: Delicate Crunchy Mexican Corn Sopes
description: A funky, crumbly, and delicious Mexican dish featuring icing sugar and papillote, perfect for a snack or light meal. This jain-friendly recipe is made with sopes and served on a lazy-susan-spinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the masa harina and icing sugar.
Gradually add in the vegetable oil and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it is smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle using a rolling pin.
Place a sheet of papillote paper on top of each circle and brush with a little water.
Top each sope with a spoonful of corn kernels, a sprinkle of salt, and a pinch of chili powder.
Fold the papillote paper over the filling to form a packet and bake for 15 minutes.
Serve the sopes hot with a squeeze of lime juice and a sprinkle of icing sugar.
